title: Isolation of diterpenoids from Caesalpinia pulcherrima wood
author: A. D. Landge*, S. R. Chaudhari and C. R. Pawar1
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Caesalpinia pulcherrima is also known as peacock flower. Caesalpinia pulcherrima (Caesalpiniaceae) is a plant found in West Indies; common throughout Sonaran deserts, naturalized in Texas. Phytochemical study of the plant reveals that the plant contains various phytoconstituents like flavonoids, sterols, triterpines etc. Two caesalpin-type furanoditerpenoids and sitisterol were isolated from the wood of Caesalpinia pulcherrima. The chemical structures were elucidated by analysis of their spectroscopic data.
